summary: Recovered docker-runtime capacity after the Gitea attachment storage exhaustion, restarted the OpenBao, Gitea, and Harbor stacks, unsealed OpenBao, and verified health endpoints. Updated the validation SBOM scanner to mount host paths for grype scans on the Gitea runner, re-ran the focused automation tests plus agent standards validation, and confirmed the latest release-bundle run completes successfully on the private Gitea main snapshot.

verification.observed: `df -h /` on docker-runtime reports 14G available (86% used) after cleanup.
OpenBao `/v1/sys/health` shows `sealed:false` and `initialized:true` on the 8201 listener.
`/api/healthz` on the private Gitea container returned `status: pass`.
The local Harbor v2 endpoint returns HTTP 401 with the registry auth challenge headers.

verification.check: SBOM scanner validation and automation slice
verification.observed: `pytest -q tests/test_sbom_scanner.py` and the Renovate automation workflow tests both succeeded, and `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards` passed.
`release-bundle.yml` run `190` concluded `success` on the private `main` snapshot.

notes: This receipt captures the post-mainline follow-up repairs that restored the Gitea release-bundle path and the SBOM validation path after the initial ADR 0297 live apply.
One stale Renovate PR sync validation run still needs a fresh replay after mainline updates, so its branch-local checkout state should be recycled once Renovate re-runs.
